title: competent-search-thing config.json
description: The application configuration file, loaded from <configDir>/config.json (Linux: ~/.config/competent-search-thing/, overridable via the COMPETENT_SEARCH_CONFIG_DIR environment variable). Source of truth: internal/config/config.go. The app itself ignores unknown keys (plain encoding/json decoding) and repairs missing or nonsensical values via Normalize; this schema is stricter than the loader on purpose, so editors catch typos and out-of-range values that the app would silently fix.

$schema.description: Optional editor hint pointing at this schema. Ignored by the app.

roots.description: Directories to index. Default: the whole filesystem, Everything-style ('/' on Linux/macOS, the system drive -- usually 'C:\' -- on Windows). Relative paths are made absolute on load; an empty or missing list falls back to the default. Symlinks are indexed as entries but never descended. Network and virtual filesystem mountpoints under a root are skipped automatically at scan time; add such a mountpoint as an explicit root to index it anyway (see the README's 'Indexing scope').

rootsVersion.description: Roots-defaults version stamp, written by the app. 0 (or absent) marks a config from before whole-filesystem indexing: on load the app upgrades roots still set to the old default (the home directory) to the whole-filesystem default, appends the missing system excludes, logs exactly what changed, sets this to 2, and rewrites the file. Customized roots are never touched by the migration. Do not edit by hand unless you want the migration to run again.

excludes.description: Patterns pruned from indexing. A pattern WITHOUT a path separator matches each entry's base name ('node_modules', '*.tmp'): matching directories are pruned, matching files skipped. A pattern WITH a separator matches the full path ('/home/*/secret'). '*' never crosses a separator and there is no '**'. An explicitly empty list means 'exclude nothing'. The same semantics apply to the initial walk, live filesystem events, and rescans. The default adds the Linux/macOS system entries (/proc, /sys, /dev, /run, /tmp, /var/tmp, lost+found) so a whole-filesystem walk skips virtual and volatile trees; on Windows the default is just the three base-name patterns.

hotkey.description: The global summon shortcut: '+'-separated tokens, case- and whitespace-insensitive, zero or more modifiers followed by exactly one key. Modifiers: ctrl/control, shift, alt/option, super/win/cmd/meta. Keys: space, tab, enter/return, esc/escape, a-z, 0-9, f1-f12, up/down/left/right. Examples: 'alt+space', 'ctrl+shift+k', 'super+space'. An invalid or unregistrable hotkey is logged and the app runs on without one.

rescanIntervalMinutes.description: Optional periodic full re-index (a safety net on top of live fsnotify updates); 0 disables the timer. Negative values are normalized to 0 by the app; this schema rejects them.

maxResults.description: Maximum number of file results one query returns. Zero or negative values are reset to the default by the app; this schema rejects them.

theme.description: The UI theme: a builtin ('dark', 'light') or the name of a user theme file at <configDir>/themes/<name>.json (see theme.schema.json). An unknown or invalid theme is logged and falls back to 'dark'. An empty value means the default.

rewrites.description: Regex rewrite rules: each rule turns a query matching its pattern into one instant top result opening the expanded URL (e.g. a pasted Jira key XY-12345 -> https://my.jira.com/XY-12345). Rules run in config order; several can match at once.

fuzzyDisabled.description: true turns the fuzzy (subsequence) name-match tier off, leaving exact/prefix/substring matching only. The default keeps fuzzy matching on; exact, prefix, and substring matches always rank above fuzzy ones either way (see the README's 'Fuzzy matching').

disabled.description: true turns the whole plugin system off, built-in providers included.

entries.description: Per-plugin overrides keyed by provider id (the built-in ids 'bangs', 'app', 'apps', 'apps-search', 'firefox-frequent' and 'firefox-tabs' work here too).

disabled.description: true turns this one provider off.

settings.description: Opaque JSON object forwarded verbatim to this plugin in every request (its 'settings' field), so plugins can be configured without editing their manifest.

disabled.description: true turns the tray icon (StatusNotifierItem) off. The default keeps it on; sessions without an SNI host (no AppIndicator extension, headless) just never show it.

frequentSitesConfig.title: Frequently-visited Firefox sites
frequentSitesConfig.description: The 'Frequent Sites' result section: pages from your own local Firefox history, read on your machine and never transmitted. A page qualifies when it was visited at least minVisitsMonth times in the past 30 days AND at least minVisitsWeek times in the past 7 days. Turn the section off via plugins.entries['firefox-frequent'].disabled. Zero or negative numbers are repaired to the defaults by the app; this schema rejects them.

minVisitsMonth.description: Minimum visits in the past 30 days (default 11 = 'more than 10 times').

minVisitsWeek.description: Minimum visits in the past 7 days (default 1).

refreshMinutes.description: How old the cached site list may get before a query kicks a background re-read of the history (default 10).

maxResults.description: Maximum results in one Frequent Sites section (default 6).

profileDir.description: Absolute path of a Firefox profile directory. When non-empty, profile discovery (classic/snap/flatpak locations) is skipped and this profile's places.sqlite is used.

openTabsConfig.title: Open Firefox tabs
openTabsConfig.description: The 'Open Tabs' result section: the tabs currently open in your own local Firefox, read from its crash-recovery session snapshot on your machine and never transmitted. The snapshot only exists while Firefox runs, so a closed browser means an empty section; private windows are never in it. Activating a result RE-OPENS the URL in the default browser -- it cannot focus the already-open tab. Turn the section off via plugins.entries['firefox-tabs'].disabled. Zero or negative numbers are repaired to the defaults by the app; this schema rejects them.

maxResults.description: Maximum results in one Open Tabs section (default 6).

profileDir.description: Absolute path of a Firefox profile directory. When non-empty, profile discovery (classic/snap/flatpak locations) is skipped and this profile's session snapshot is used. Same semantics as frequentSites.profileDir; the two default to one shared discovery.

items.description: Exactly one character that is not a letter, digit, or space.

sigils.description: Characters that may start a bang query. Invalid sigils are logged and skipped by the app; an empty or all-invalid list falls back to the defaults.

aliases.description: Extra names mapped onto registered bangs, e.g. {"math": "calc"} makes '!math' target the plugin that registered 'calc'. Keys are lowercased on load; only [a-z0-9_-] names can ever be typed. An alias pointing at a bang that never gets registered is ignored.

^[a-z0-9_-]+$.description: The registered bang name this alias resolves to.

disabled.description: true turns the system-stats row's sampler off (the row then shows placeholders only). The default keeps it on; sampling happens only while the bar is visible, and metrics without a source on this machine (no GPU busy file, non-Linux platforms) degrade to placeholders by themselves.

persistDisabled.description: true keeps the query history in memory only: nothing is read from or written to <configDir>/history.json. In-session Up/Down recall still works; the list is simply forgotten when the app quits. The default persists the newest 100 committed queries to that file (created 0600).

previewConfig.title: Preview pane configuration
previewConfig.description: The right-hand preview pane showing the selected result (text with syntax highlighting, image thumbnails, directory listings, metadata; explicit-trigger Kagi web search and OpenAI answers). Off by default. Zero or negative numbers and an empty openai.model are repaired to the defaults by the app; this schema rejects them.

enabled.description: true turns the preview pane on and widens the window to windowWidth x windowHeight. Read once at startup; the window size cannot change while the app runs.

windowWidth.description: Window width in pixels while the pane is enabled (default 1600). Ignored when enabled is false.

windowHeight.description: Window height in pixels while the pane is enabled (default 800). Ignored when enabled is false.

textMaxKB.description: How much of a text file one preview reads, in KiB (default 256); longer files are truncated with a marker.

imageMaxEdge.description: An image thumbnail's longest edge in pixels (default 800); larger sources are downscaled.

dirMaxEntries.description: Maximum entries in one directory listing preview (default 200).

apiKey.description: SECRET: the Kagi Search API token. Passed through verbatim and never logged; keep config.json private when set. Empty means 'use the KAGI_API_KEY environment variable, if set'; with neither, the web-search preview stays unavailable.

maxResults.description: Maximum results in one web-search preview (default 8).

apiKey.description: SECRET: the OpenAI API key. Passed through verbatim and never logged; keep config.json private when set. Empty means 'use the OPENAI_API_KEY environment variable, if set'; with neither, the answer preview stays unavailable.

model.description: The model answering (default 'gpt-5-mini'). An empty value is repaired to the default by the app; this schema rejects it.

translucent.description: true requests a per-pixel-alpha (RGBA) window so the area outside the bar's rounded corners is truly see-through instead of a squared-off opaque fill. REQUIRES a running compositor: every Wayland session qualifies, but on X11 one must be running when the app starts (a bare window manager like stock openbox shows solid black corners instead). The default keeps the window opaque -- exactly the previous behavior.

width.description: The bar window's width in pixels (default 780). The window size is fixed at startup (the bar is not resizable); changes take effect on the next launch. Zero, negative, or missing values get the default and positive values below 320 are raised to that floor by the app; this schema rejects them.

height.description: The bar window's height in pixels (default 550). Same repair rules as width, with a 240 floor. A taller window shows more result rows before scrolling; how many results a query returns stays governed by the top-level maxResults.

name.description: Display name, shown as the result's subtitle.

pattern.description: Go regexp (RE2: no backtracking, linear time) matched against the trimmed query. FULL-MATCH by default: compiled as ^(?:pattern)$ unless you anchor it yourself (a leading ^ or trailing $ disables the wrapping); write looser rules with explicit .* . An invalid pattern is logged once at startup and the rule is skipped.

replacement.description: URL template expanded with the stdlib capture-group syntax: $1, ${name}, $$ for a literal $. Must expand to an absolute http(s) URL with a host or the result is dropped (open_url is the only action rewrites can produce).

title.description: Optional result title template (same expansion); defaults to the expanded URL.

icon.description: Optional icon (builtin name or literal glyph); defaults to "link".

disabled.description: Turns the rule off without deleting it.
